title: pedicularis foliosa subsp. oederi
intro: Pedicularis foliosa subsp. oederi, also known as Oeder's lousewort, is a subspecies of the Pedicularis foliosa plant, which is a type of flowering plant belonging to the borage family. Traditionally, Oeder's lousewort has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Oeder's lousewort include its ability to soothe and calm the nervous system, making it a potential treatment for anxiety and insomnia. In herbal preparations, Oeder's lousewort is typically dried and made into tea, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
